expectations.text: Report contains a Summary section with counts of confirmed vs disputed issues
expectations.evidence: No formal Summary section. The report jumps straight into 'Concern 1' without an overview. There is a summary table at the very end (lines 149-154) but it's not at the top and doesn't count confirmed vs disputed.

expectations.text: Each issue gets a verdict from the taxonomy (Confirmed/Reseveritied/Partially valid/Disputed/Needs more context)
expectations.evidence: Uses non-standard verdict labels: 'PARTIALLY VALID', 'NOT A REAL PROBLEM -- You are overthinking this', 'VALID'. Does not use the defined taxonomy terms.

expectations.text: Each verdict includes investigation evidence citing specific file paths and line numbers
expectations.evidence: Extensive evidence throughout. Issue 1 cites main.py line 3, profile_scan.py line 23, iceburger.py. Issue 2 cites custom_loader.py lines 147-184. Issue 3 cites main.py lines 283, 211-212, 10, 243, 215, 246, 190.

expectations.text: Each issue includes a fix assessment evaluating or proposing solutions
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 recommends moving AWS_PROFILE inside __main__. Issue 2 notes no fix needed but flags performance concern. Issue 3 provides specific fixes for each sub-issue.

expectations.text: Investigation reads actual code and traces logic rather than just restating the input concerns
expectations.evidence: Thorough code reading throughout. Issue 2 traces the full data flow and correctly identifies it's all in-memory. Issue 3 identifies 7 sub-issues (3a-3g) from reading the actual code.

expectations.text: Searches for issues proactively when input is imprecise rather than refusing or asking for more info
expectations.evidence: All 3 vague concerns investigated proactively. Found sub-issues in the training loop beyond what was asked. However, did not formally flag new issues in a separate section.
